core_mechanism: high-velocity (large-parameter) asymptotic reduction of a coherent localized nonlinear pulse scattering off a point-like potential defect, splitting the outgoing wave into explicit reflected and transmitted coherent components governed by effective linear reflection/transmission coefficients, with an asymptotically vanishing dispersive/trapped remainder
brief_reason: The abstract studies a soliton hitting an attractive delta-function potential and shows that in the high-velocity limit it splits into analytically explicit reflected and transmitted soliton parts plus a small dispersive remainder, with the bound-state-trapped fraction vanishing, i.e. a fast-scattering perturbative decomposition into transmission/reflection coefficients.
